sentence: וַיֹּאמֶר אֱלֹהִים יְהִי רָקִיעַ בְּתוֹךְ הַמָּיִם וִיהִי מַבְדִּיל בֵּין מַיִם לָמָיִם

grammatical-notes.רָקִיעַ: Noun. From 'raqa' (to hammer/beat out). An expanse or firmament.
grammatical-notes.וִיהִי: Conjunction + Qal Jussive. Command for ongoing state.
grammatical-notes.מַבְדִּיל: Hiphil Participle. From 'badal'. 'In a state of causing separation'. Continuous causative agency.

dictionary-meaning.רָקִיעַ: A hammered-out expanse (a stretched-out layer of separation) — disclosing the physical texture of a 'beaten out' sheet that provides structural separation (Rule 21).
dictionary-meaning.מַבְדִּיל: Remaining in a state of establishing a sovereign and fixed boundary — disclosing the continuous Hiphil Participle of 'badal' (Rule 9, 11).
dictionary-meaning.בְּתוֹךְ: Within the presence and center of — disclosing the positional depth of the expanse (Rule 1).
dictionary-meaning.מַיִם: Waters — the primordial material being divided. Left anarthrous in the first instance to signify the 'nature' of the fluid being separated (Rule 75).

רקיע.chosen: hammered-out expanse (a stretched-out layer of separation)
רקיע.reason: The Hebrew root 'raqa' (to beat out) necessitates a term that conveys the physical, structural 'stretched' quality of the sky.

main-translation: ***Then Elohim decreed***, '***Let existence be granted to a hammered-out expanse (a stretched-out layer of separation) within the center of the waters, and let it remain in a state of establishing a sovereign and fixed boundary between waters and the waters.***'

main-translation-explanation.hammered-out expanse: INTERNAL ADVERSARIAL TRIAL: Discloses the physical root 'raqa' (to beat out) showing the author's intent of a structural layer (Rule 21).

rejected-translations.reason: Archaic (Rule 4) and loses the 'active stretching' meaning of the Hebrew root.
Too abstract; masks the material texture of the 'beaten out' expanse.
